source_refs.html: /Users/toy/English/躺着学/躺着学阅读/8055_Passage 3 Pottery Production in Ancient Akrotiri.html

passages.title: Pottery Production in Ancient Akrotiri
passages.source_html:
Pottery Production in Ancient Akrotiri


A


Excavations at the site of prehistoric Akrotiri, on the coast of the Aegean Sea, have revealed much about the technical aspects of pottery manufacture, indisputably one of the basic industries of this Greek city. However, considerably less is known about the socio-economic context and the way production was organised.


B


The bulk of pottery found at Akrotiri is locally made and dates from the late fifteenth century BC. It clearly fulfilled a vast range of the settlement’s requirements: more than fifty different types of pots can be distinguished. The pottery found includes a wide variety of functional types like storage jars, smaller containers, pouring vessels, cooking pots, drinking vessels and so on, which all relate to specific activities and which would have been made and distributed with those activities in mind. Given a large number of shapes produced and the relatively high degree of standardisation, it has generally been assumed that most, if not all, of Akrotiri pottery, was produced by specialised craftsmen in a non-domestic context. Unfortunately, neither the potters’ workshops nor kilns have been found within the excavated area. The reason may be that the ceramic workshops were located on the periphery of the site, which has not yet been excavated. In any event, the ubiquity of the pottery, and the consistent repetition of the same types in different sizes suggest production on an industrial scale.


C


The Akrotirian potters seem to have responded to pressures beyond their households, namely to the increasing complexity of regional distribution and exchange systems. We can imagine them as full-time craftsmen working permanently in a high production-rate craft such as pottery manufacture, and supporting themselves entirely from the proceeds of their craft. In view of the above, one can begin to speak in terms of mass-produced pottery and the existence of organised workshops of craftsmen during the period 1550-1500 BC. Yet, how pottery production was organised at Akrotiri remains an open question, as there is no real documentary evidence. Our entire knowledge comes from the ceramic material itself, and the tentative conclusions which can be drawn from it.


D


The invention of units of quantity and of a numerical system to count them was of capital importance of an exchange-geared society such as that of Akrotiri. In spite of the absence of any written records, the archaeological evidence reveals that concepts of measurements, both weight and number, had been formulated. Standard measures may already have been in operation, such as those evidenced by a graduated series of lead weights – made in disc form – found at the site. The existence of units of capacity in Late Bronze Age times is also evidenced, by the notation of units of a liquid measure for wine on excavated containers.


E


It must be recognised that the function of pottery vessels plays a very important role in determining their characteristics. The intended function affects the choice of clay, the production technique, and the shape and the size of the pots. For example, large storage jars would be needed to store commodities, whereas smaller containers would be used for transport. In fact, the length of a man’s arm limits the size of a smaller pot to a capacity of about twenty litres; that is also the maximum a man can comfortably carry.


F


The various sizes of container would thus represent standard quantities of a commodity, which is a fundamental element in the function of exchange. Akrotirian merchants handling a commodity such as wine would have been able to determine easily the amount of wine they were transporting from the number of containers they carried in their ships since the capacity of each container was known to be 14-18 litres. (We could draw a parallel here with the current practice in Greece of selling oil in 17-kilogram tins.)


G


We may, therefore, assume that the shape, capacity, and, sometimes decoration of vessels are indicative of the commodity contained by them. Since individual transactions would normally involve different quantities of a given commodity, a range of ‘standardised’ types of the vessel would be needed to meet traders’ requirements.


H


In trying to reconstruct systems of capacity by measuring the volume of excavated pottery, a rather generous range of tolerances must be allowed. It seems possible that the potters of that time had specific sizes of the vessel in mind, and tried to reproduce them using a specific type and amount of clay. However, it would be quite difficult for them to achieve the exact size required every time, without any mechanical means of regulating symmetry and wall thickness, and some potters would be more skilled than others. In addition, variations in the repetition of types and size may also occur because of unforeseen circumstances during the throwing process. For instance, instead of destroying the entire pot if the clay in the rim contained a piece of grit, a potter might produce a smaller pot by simply cutting off the rim. Even where there is no noticeable external difference between pots meant to contain the same quantity of a commodity, differences in their capacity can actually reach one or two litres. In one case the deviation from the required size appears to be as much as 10-20 per cent.


I 


The establishment of regular trade routes within the Aegean led to increased movement of goods; consequently, a regular exchange of local, luxury and surplus goods, including metals, would have become feasible as a result of the advances in transport technology. The increased demand for standardised exchanges, inextricably linked to commercial transactions, might have been one of the main factors which led to the standardisation of pottery production. Thus, the whole network of ceramic production and exchange would have depended on specific regional economic conditions and would reflect the socio-economic structure of prehistoric Akrotiri.

groups.title: Questions 27-40
groups.source_html:
Questions 27-28

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

27

What does the writer say about of pottery excavated at Akrotiri?

正确答案： B. They would have met a big variety of needs.

原文答案句：The bulk of pottery found at Akrotiri is locally made and dates from the late fifteenth century BC. It clearly fulfilled a vast range of the settlement’s requirements: more than fifty different types of pots can be distinguished.


对应段落：B段


审题：题目问的是关于在Akrotiri发掘的陶器，作者说了什么。


定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"pottery excavated at Akrotiri"可以定位到文章的B段，该段提到了在Akrotiri发掘的陶器的情况。


解题思路：根据B段第一句话，可以得知在Akrotiri发掘的陶器大部分是本地制造的，并且可以满足该定居点的大量需求，有超过五十种不同的陶器类型可以被区分。这表明这些陶器可以满足各种各样的需求，与选项B"它们将满足大量需求"相符。因此，正确答案是B。


28

The assumption that pottery from Akrotiri was produced by specialists is partly based on

正确答案： D. the wide range of shapes represented.

原文答案句：The bulk of pottery found at Akrotiri is locally made and dates from the late fifteenth century BC. It clearly fulfilled a vast range of the settlement’s requirements: more than fifty different types of pots can be distinguished.


对应段落：B段


审题：题目问的是关于在Akrotiri发掘的陶器，作者说了什么。


定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"pottery excavated at Akrotiri"可以定位到文章的B段，该段提到了在Akrotiri发掘的陶器的情况。


解题思路：根据B段第一句话，可以得知在Akrotiri发掘的陶器大部分是本地制造的，并且可以满足该定居点的大量需求，有超过五十种不同的陶器类型可以被区分。这表明这些陶器可以满足各种各样的需求，与选项B"它们将满足大量需求"相符。因此，正确答案是B。


Questions 29-32

Complete each sentence with the correct ending.

Options (Word Bank):
A. the discovery of a collection of metal discs.
B. the size and type of sailing ships in use.
C. variations in the exact shape and thickness of similar containers.
D. the physical characteristics of workmen.
E. marks found on wine containers.
F. the variety of commodities for which they would have been used.
Question / ParagraphABCDEF
29

The assumption that standard units of weight were in use could be based on ________
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：In spite of the absence of any written records, the archaeological evidence reveals that concepts of measurements, both weight and number, had been formulated. Standard measures may already have been in operation, such as those evidenced by a graduated series of lead weights – made in disc form – found at the site.  


对应段落：D段  


审题：题目问的是“关于标准重量单位的假设可能基于什么证据”。  


定位关键信息：通过题干中的"standard units of weight"和"based on"定位到D段，该段提到"concepts of measurements, both weight and number, had been formulated"以及"Standard measures may already have been in operation, such as those evidenced by a graduated series of lead weights – made in disc form – found at the site."  


解题思路：原文中明确提到标准重量单位的假设是基于在遗址中发现的一系列铅制圆盘形砝码（lead weights – made in disc form），这些砝码的存在证明了当时已经存在标准重量单位的概念。因此，答案为A（the discovery of a collection of metal discs）。
30

Evidence of the use of standard units of the volume is provided by ________
✅ 正确答案：E

原文答案句：The existence of units of capacity in Late Bronze Age times is also evidenced, by the notation of units of a liquid measure for wine on excavated containers.


对应段落：D段


审题：题目问的是“证据表明使用了标准的体积单位”。

定位关键信息：利用题目的“standard units of volume”和“evidence”这两个关键词，在文章中定位到D段，该段明确提到了“units of capacity”和“evidence”。


解题思路：原文中D段提到“units of capacity in Late Bronze Age times is also evidenced”，这与题目中的“standard units of volume”和“evidence”相对应。因此，可以确定答案为D段中提到的“notation of units of a liquid measure for wine on excavated containers”。
31

The size of certain types of containers would have been restricted by ________
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：In fact, the length of a man’s arm limits the size of a smaller pot to a capacity of about twenty litres; that is also the maximum a man can comfortably carry.


对应段落：E段


审题：题目问的是某种容器的尺寸会受到什么限制。


定位关键信息：根据题干中的"size of certain types of containers"和"restricted by"可以定位到E段中关于容器尺寸限制的描述。


解题思路：原文中明确提到"the length of a man’s arm limits the size of a smaller pot"，即人的手臂长度限制了小容器的尺寸，这直接对应了题目中"size of certain types of containers would have been restricted by"的描述。因此，答案为D。
32

Attempts to identify the intended capacity of containers are complicated by ________
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：In trying to reconstruct systems of capacity by measuring the volume of excavated pottery, a rather generous range of tolerances must be allowed.


对应段落：H段


审题：题目问的是“试图确定容器预期容量所面临的问题是什么”，即容器容量识别过程中的复杂性。


定位关键信息：根据题干中的"identify the intended capacity of containers"和"complicated by"可以定位到H段首句，该句提到了"reconstruct systems of capacity"和"generous range of tolerances"。


解题思路：H段首句明确指出，在通过测量出土陶器体积来重建容量系统时，必须允许相当大的容差范围。这表明容器容量识别过程因允许的容差范围较大而变得复杂，即无法精确确定每个容器的实际容量。原文中的"a rather generous range of tolerances must be allowed"直接对应题目中的"complicated by"，说明容差范围的存在使得容量识别复杂化。因此，答案为C。

Question33-38

Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer in the passage? Choose YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer, NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer, NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this.

33

There are plans to excavate new areas of the archaeological site the near future.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：The reason may be that the ceramic workshops were located on the periphery of the site, which has not yet been excavated.


对应段落：B段


审题：题目问的是“考古遗址计划在不久的将来开挖新区域”，即询问作者是否提到有开挖新区域的计划。


定位关键信息：通过题目中的“excavate new areas”和“in the near future”定位到B段中“which has not yet been excavated”，这句话表明新区域尚未被发掘，但并未提及是否有计划在不久的将来进行开挖。


解题思路：原文提到新区域尚未被发掘，但并未提及任何关于未来开挖的计划或安排。因此，无法确定作者是否认为有计划在不久的将来开挖新区域，所以答案为“Not Given”。

34

Some of the evidence concerning pottery production in ancient Akrotiri comes from written records.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：In spite of the absence of any written records, the archaeological evidence reveals that concepts of measurements, both weight and number, had been formulated.


对应段落：D段


审题：题目问的是关于古代阿克罗蒂里陶器生产的部分证据是否来自书面记录。


定位关键信息：题目中的关键词是"written records"，在文章中定位到D段，找到含有"written records"或相关表述的句子。


解题思路：根据D段原文句子"In spite of the absence of any written records, the archaeological evidence reveals that concepts of measurements, both weight and number, had been formulated."可知，文章明确提到没有书面记录，陶器生产的证据来自考古发现而非书面记录。因此，题目说法与作者观点相反，应选择"No"。


35

Pots for transporting liquids would have held no more than about 20 litres.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：For example, large storage jars would be needed to store commodities, whereas smaller containers would be used for transport. In fact, the length of a man’s arm limits the size of a smaller pot to a capacity of about twenty litres; that is also the maximum a man can comfortably carry.


对应段落：E段


审题：题目问的是“用于运输液体的罐子容量不超过大约20升”，需要判断作者是否认同这一说法。


定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“transporting liquids”和“no more than about 20 litres”定位到E段。原文提到“smaller containers would be used for transport”和“the length of a man’s arm limits the size of a smaller pot to a capacity of about twenty litres”，说明用于运输的小型容器容量约为20升。


解题思路：原文明确指出用于运输的容器（smaller containers）容量约为20升，且这是由人的臂长限制的。题目中的“no more than about 20 litres”与原文“capacity of about twenty litres”一致，因此作者认同这一说法。答案为A（Yes）。

36

It would have been hard for merchants to calculate how much wine was on their ships.

正确答案： B. NO

原文答案句：The capacity of each container was known to be 14-18 litres. (F段)


对应段落：F段


审题：题目问的是商人是否很难计算出船上葡萄酒的数量。


定位关键信息：利用题干中的关键词"merchants"和"calculate how much wine"定位到F段，该段提到商人可以通过容器数量和已知容量来计算葡萄酒的数量。


解题思路：原文明确指出商人可以通过容器数量和已知容量（14-18升）来计算葡萄酒的数量，因此商人实际上可以很容易地计算出葡萄酒的数量，与题干说法相反。故答案为B（No）。

37

The capacity of containers intended to hold the same amounts differed by up to 20 per cent.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：Even where there is no noticeable external difference between pots meant to contain the same quantity of a commodity, differences in their capacity can actually reach one or two litres. In one case the deviation from the required size appears to be as much as 10-20 per cent.


对应段落：H段


审题：题目问的是“用于盛装相同数量的容器的容量差异可达20%”，需要判断作者是否同意这一说法。


定位关键信息：通过题干中的“containers intended to hold the same amounts”和“differed by up to 20 per cent”可以定位到H段中“pots meant to contain the same quantity of a commodity”和“deviation from the required size appears to be as much as 10-20 per cent”。


解题思路：原文H段明确提到，即使在外观上没有明显差异的用于盛装相同数量商品的容器，其容量差异实际上可以达到一到两升，并且在一种情况下，与所需尺寸的偏差高达10-20%。这与题目中的说法完全一致，因此作者同意这一观点，答案为A（Yes）。

38

Regular trading of goods around the Aegean would have led to the general standardisation of quantities.

正确答案： A. YES

原文答案句：The increased demand for standardised exchanges, inextricably linked to commercial transactions, might have been one of the main factors which led to the standardisation of pottery production.


对应段落：I段


审题：题目问的是“爱琴海地区的商品常规贸易是否会导致数量的普遍标准化”，需要判断作者是否认同这一观点。


定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"Regular trading of goods around the Aegean"和"standardisation of quantities"可以定位到I段，该段提到"increased demand for standardised exchanges"和"standardisation of pottery production"，这与题目中的"standardisation of quantities"相关。


解题思路：原文中I段提到，对标准化交换的日益增长的需求，与商业交易密不可分，可能是导致陶器生产标准化的主要原因之一。这与题目中的"Regular trading of goods around the Aegean would have led to the general standardisation of quantities"意思一致，即爱琴海地区的商品常规贸易会导致数量的普遍标准化。因此，作者认同这一观点，答案为A（Yes）。

Question39-40

Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

39

What does the writer say about the standardisation of container sizes?

正确答案： A. Containers which looked the same from the outside often varied in capacity.

原文答案句：The increased demand for standardised exchanges, inextricably linked to commercial transactions, might have been one of the main factors which led to the standardisation of pottery production.


对应段落：I段


审题：题目问的是“爱琴海地区的商品常规贸易是否会导致数量的普遍标准化”，需要判断作者是否认同这一观点。


定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"Regular trading of goods around the Aegean"和"standardisation of quantities"可以定位到I段，该段提到"increased demand for standardised exchanges"和"standardisation of pottery production"，这与题目中的"standardisation of quantities"相关。


解题思路：原文中I段提到，对标准化交换的日益增长的需求，与商业交易密不可分，可能是导致陶器生产标准化的主要原因之一。这与题目中的"Regular trading of goods around the Aegean would have led to the general standardisation of quantities"意思一致，即爱琴海地区的商品常规贸易会导致数量的普遍标准化。因此，作者认同这一观点，答案为A（Yes）。


40

What is probably the main purpose of the passage?

正确答案： B. To suggest how features of pottery production at Akrotiri reflected other developments in the region

原文答案句：The bulk of pottery found at Akrotiri is locally made and dates from the late fifteenth century BC. It clearly fulfilled a vast range of the settlement’s requirements: more than fifty different types of pots can be distinguished. The pottery found includes a wide variety of functional types like storage jars, smaller containers, pouring vessels, cooking pots, drinking vessels and so on, which all relate to specific activities and which would have been made and distributed with those activities in mind. Given a large number of shapes produced and the relatively high degree of standardisation, it has generally been assumed that most, if not all, of Akrotiri pottery, was produced by specialised craftsmen in a non-domestic context. Unfortunately, neither the potters’ workshops nor kilns have been found within the excavated area. The reason may be that the ceramic workshops were located on the periphery of the site, which has not yet been excavated. In any event, the ubiquity of the pottery, and the consistent repetition of the same types in different sizes suggest production on an industrial scale.


对应段落：B段


审题：题目问的是文章的主要目的是什么。


定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"main purpose"可以定位到文章中关于陶器生产的目的和意义的描述。


解题思路：根据原文中的句子，可以看出文章主要讨论的是阿克罗蒂里出土的陶器的特征和生产方式，以及这些特征如何反映了当时的社会经济状况。文章并没有直接评价陶器的质量，也没有详细描述陶器制作技能的发展，更没有具体描述储存和运输家用物品的方法。因此，文章的主要目的是探讨阿克罗蒂里陶器生产的特征如何反映了其他地区的发展。
